title: Connect desktop Codex into Agent Community as one new identity without modifying community
objective: Use the existing community integration model as reference, but do not modify the community backend, protocol, workflow engine, or UI. The only task is to make this Codex-side capability obtain one valid community identity, join a target group, and prove that it can correctly receive community-directed work and send compliant responses back through the community channel.

target_outcome.identity_goal: Desktop Codex side obtains exactly one new community identity.
target_outcome.integration_goal: That identity can join a target group, receive community-originated work, and send correct community responses.
target_outcome.boundary_goal: No community-side code or protocol is changed.

mandatory_reference_order.why: Read first to understand the intended behavior and deployment assumptions of a community-connected agent.
Read second to understand the exact onboarding artifacts, identity env vars, route registration behavior, and state file locations.
Read third to understand the current community-facing runtime contract: state file, webhook path, send path, socket/listen model, outbound send path, and auth usage.
Read fourth to identify the minimum environment bundle required for one community-connected identity.
Read fifth only if direct reuse of the OpenClaw skill model is not appropriate. This is the prior HTTP-controller pattern to reference for a thin Codex-community bridge.
Use as the official source for workspace-scoped skill behavior.
Use to avoid incorrectly treating OpenClaw sub-agents as fully isolated independent agents.
Use to distinguish independent agents from sub-agent sessions.

must_not_research_beyond: Do not scan the community backend repo to look for a place to patch community behavior.
Do not investigate community UI implementation for this task.
Do not investigate workflow templates or group protocol redesign for this task.

Do not perform broad repo-wide archaeology outside the mandatory references unless one of the mandatory references directly points to an additional file needed to complete the one-identity integration.

allowed_work_areas.scope: Reference and, only if necessary, adapt agent-side integration behavior for Codex-side use. Do not broaden this into multi-identity support.
Reference or reuse the prior direct-control bridge pattern if a thin Codex-community bridge is the cleaner route.
Write evidence, implementation notes, onboarding proof, and closeout.

forbidden_work_areas.scope: Do not modify any backend/community/frontend code here.
Do not treat this task as a community boundary or workflow repair task.
Do not turn this into a workflow deployment task.

starting_points: First decide whether the correct route is direct reuse/adaptation of CommunityIntegrationSkill behavior or a thinner Codex-community bridge.
Then define one Codex-side identity bundle: handle, local state file, auth token storage, outbound identity binding, and inbound task source.
Then implement only the minimum local bridge/onboarding needed for one identity.
Then prove one round-trip: community task/message in, Codex-side handling, community-compliant response out.
assumptions: This thread may add or modify only Codex-side adapter/bridge code or local support scripts.
This task is explicitly single-identity only.
If direct reuse of CommunityIntegrationSkill is awkward for desktop Codex, a thin bridge is acceptable as long as the community-facing contract remains correct.

required_work.description: Choose and justify the correct integration route based only on the mandatory references: direct skill adaptation or thin bridge.
Define the one Codex-connected community identity bundle: handle/name, local state path, auth material storage, outbound identity binding, and target group join path.
Implement the minimum local integration needed for one Codex identity to register or reuse an identity, join the target group, receive community-directed work, and send compliant messages back.
Run onboarding for exactly one new Codex-connected identity and capture identity/state evidence.
Prove the new Codex-connected identity can receive a community-originated task/message and send a structurally correct response back.
Write a closeout that states what was built, what identity was created, what message path was used, and why no community-side modification was required.

do_not: Do not modify the community backend code.
Do not modify community protocol definitions.
Do not modify the workflow engine or group-session logic.
Do not modify community UI code.
Do not broaden the task into multi-identity or multi-agent support.
Do not solve identity by trusting a payload self-declared id without authenticated identity binding.
working_principles: Community remains the communication bus and identity authority.
Desktop Codex remains an external execution capability.
If a bridge is required, keep it thin: identity/auth, message translation, task handoff, result return.
Prefer the smallest correct solution that yields one real community identity and one proven round-trip.
Use the mandatory references first and stop broad repo exploration.
acceptance: A clear integration route is chosen and justified with the listed references.
Exactly one new Codex-connected community identity is onboarded or reused successfully.
That identity can be shown as a valid community member/agent identity.
No community backend/protocol/UI code was modified.

deliverables.description: Evidence folder for route choice, implementation, onboarding proof, round-trip proof, and final closeout.
Detailed execution log for this thread.
